title: Presentations deck slides.json schema
description: DETERMINISTIC deck pipeline input. An AI agent produces THIS file from the source material (transcript/brief). build_deck.py then renders it with ZERO further AI judgement: it composes a KIE.ai prompt per slide (photographic scene + the EXACT English copy + the mandatory English/Latin-only pin), calls KIE.ai gpt-image-2, downloads one full-bleed 16:9 PNG per slide, and assembles a .pptx. The agent NEVER generates images itself. All wording that must appear on a slide must be written, spelled correctly, in the 'copy' array — KIE renders that text INTO the image.

slide.description: 1-based slide ordinal. Determines slide order in the deck AND the render filename (slide-01.png, slide-02.png, ...). Must be unique and contiguous starting at 1.

scene.description: Photographic scene description for the background image — what is literally in frame (subject, setting, lighting, mood, color palette, camera framing). Describe a PHOTOGRAPH, not a slide. Do NOT put the slide copy here; copy goes in the 'copy' array. Example: 'A confident Black woman entrepreneur standing in a sunlit modern office, soft morning light from a large window behind her, warm neutral palette, shallow depth of field, shot on 85mm, professional editorial photography.'

copy.description: The EXACT text that must appear rendered on the slide, in reading order. Index 0 is treated as the HEADLINE; remaining entries are subheads/body lines. Spell every word correctly letter-for-letter; KIE renders these verbatim into the image. Keep lines short (slide copy, not paragraphs). Example: ["Northwind Co", "Three moves that doubled our pipeline in 90 days"].

logo.description: Optional brand/logo wordmark to render on the slide (e.g. 'NORTHWIND CO'). If present, build_deck.py instructs KIE to place this wordmark as a small clean lockup (typically a corner). Omit if the deck has no per-slide logo. NOTE: this is a TEXT wordmark rendered by KIE, not an image-to-image logo placement; the deterministic t2i pipeline does not pass logo image files.

layout.description: Optional human-readable hint for WHERE the text sits and how the composition is arranged, fed into the KIE prompt (e.g. 'headline lower-left over dark gradient, subhead beneath, logo top-right', 'centered hero title, copy stacked in lower third'). If omitted, build_deck.py uses a safe default: headline upper area, subhead/body in the lower third, high-contrast legible placement.

mode.description: Image generation mode. The DETERMINISTIC pipeline uses ONLY 't2i' (gpt-image-2-text-to-image) — the copy is rendered into a freshly generated photographic scene. (Image-to-image logo placement remains available via the separate kie_generate.py helper but is out of scope for build_deck.py.) Defaults to 't2i' if omitted.

examples.scene: A confident Black woman entrepreneur standing in a sunlit modern office, soft morning light from a large window behind her, warm neutral palette, shallow depth of field, shot on 85mm lens, professional editorial photography, cinematic.
copy: Three moves that doubled our pipeline in 90 days
examples.layout: headline lower-left over a soft dark gradient, subhead directly beneath the headline, logo wordmark small in the top-right corner

examples.scene: An overhead flat-lay of a clean modern desk with a laptop showing a rising line graph, a coffee cup, and a notebook, bright natural daylight, minimalist, lots of negative space on the right side, professional product photography.
copy: Most pipelines leak at the follow-up stage — not the lead stage
examples.layout: headline and subhead stacked in the right-side negative space, dark text on the light background, logo wordmark small in the bottom-right corner
